Off Calendar Oct-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jeffrey Farrow, Esq., for defendant and moving party: John Arthur Sebastinelli, Patricia Sebastinelli and Michelman & Robinson, LLP. appearing by telephone
Joshua Arce, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Kearny Street Law Offices, Inc.
Robert Shatzko, Esq., for Cross-defendant and opposing party: Kearny Street Law Offices, Inc.

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant's motion for evidence, issue, or terminating sanctions for violation of court order to produce documents as to Raul Aguilar and Aguilar Law Offices is granted in part and denied in part. The motion for evidentiary, issue, or terminating sanctions is denied. The motion for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $1,865. Defendant shall fully comply with the previous order within 5 days. The monetary sanctions shall be paid within 30 days. If no documents, plaintiff shall submit a declaration describing his search for responsive records. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Appearances:
Raul Aguilar of Aguilar Law Offices (982-9500) for plaintiffs Raul Aguilar and the Aguilar Law Offices
Jeffrey Farrow of Michelman & Robinson (714-557-7990) appearing via Court Call for defendant John Sebastinelli

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS RAUL AGUILAR AND AGUILAR LAW OFFICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Raul Aguilar of Aguilar Law Offices (982-9500) for plaintiffs and moving parties
Jeffrey Farrow of Michelman & Robinson (714-557-7990) appearing via Court Call for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof. Under James v. Ahmanson & Co. (1969) 1 Cal. 3d 93, 107, Sebastinelli may state a claim for injury to his personal interests as contrasted with corporate interests. Aguilar's declaration fails to demonstrate that Sebastinelli's individual interests were not adversely affected by the wrongs alleged and Sebastinelli asserts he is suing for personal injuries. This also raises a triable issue of fact on this issue. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452

Motion For Clarification Of Court'S Order Of December 3, 2007
Off Calendar Dec-17-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR CLARIFICATION OF COURT'S DECEMBER 3, 2007 ORDER.
APPEARANCES: RAUL V. AGUILAR, ESQUIRE (AGUILAR LAW OFFICES, 415.982.9500) APPEARING ON HIS OWN BEHALF. ROBIN JAMES, ESQUIRE (MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, 415.882.7770) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT SEBASTINELLI.
RULING: DISCUSSED AND THE COURT INDICATES THAT IN THE DECEMBER 3,2007 ORDER, WHAT THE COURT WAS DETERMINING WAS THAT THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED ESTABLISHED THE RIGHT OF MR. AGUILAR TO MAKE A CLAIM BASED ON HIS PAYMENT OF THE WELLS FARGO LINE OF CREDIT. AS STATED IN THE ORDER, THE ISSUE OF SUBROGATION INVOLVES ONLY AGUILAR'S RIGHT TO DEMAND REPAYMENT. ANY OFFSETS FOR IMPROPER USE OF THE LINE OF CREDIT CAN BE DETERMINED THROUGH DISCOVERY. PUT ANOTHER WAY, COURT WAS SIMPLY FINDING THAT MR. SEBASTINELLI HAD A DUTY TO PAY MR. AGUILAR SOME MONEY BASED ON MR. AGUILAR'S PAYMENT OF THE WELLS FARGO LINE OF CREDIT, BUT THE COURT WAS MAKING NO DETERMINATION AS TO THE AMOUNT BECAUSE THAT WAS A DISPUTED FACT. MS. JAMES TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR 7433

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike And Special Motion To Strike Pusuant To Cco 425.16 And Request For Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,475.00;
Off Calendar Dec-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robin James, Esq. of Michelman & Robinson, 415-882-7770 for defendant and moving party Michelman & Robinson
Joshua Arce, Esq. of Brightline Defense Project, 415-837-0600 for plantiff and opposing party Kearney Street Law Offices

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
CROSS COMPLAINANT MICHELMAN & ROBISON'S Notice Of Special Motion To Strike And Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Cco 425.16 And Request For Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,475.00; THE MOTION IS DENIED. IN TRADE SECRET CASES, IT IS REASONABLE TO EXPECT THAT THE NEW EMPLOYER WILL BE A DEFENDANT. ONE INTERPRETATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CONFLICT IS THAT PLAINTIFF PROCEEDED WITH CAUTION IN NAMING M & R, WAITING UNTIL DISCOVERY SUPPORTED PLAINTIFF'S POSITION. THE OTHER ALTERNATIVE IS TO ACCEPT M & R'S FACTUAL SCENARIO, IN PARTICULAR THE AGUILAR E-MAIL OF SEPT. 6, 2006. ASSUMING M & R HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF, IN FACT, CAN PROVE ITS CASE AGAINST M & R. ALTHOUGH PORTIONS OF THE AGUILAR DECLARATION CONSIST OF ARGUMENT AND ARE ADMISSABLE, THE INFERENCES LINKING THE EVIDENCE CONSTITUTE APPROPRIATE ARGUMENT FOR THE COURT TO CONSIDER IN EVALUATION THE ABILITY OF PLAINTIFF TO PROVE ITS CASE. THE COURT TOOK ATTORNEY'S FEES UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600. (302/PJM/AY)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Joshua Arce, Esq. 982-5100 for pltf & opposing party; Robin James, Esq. of Michelman & Robinson, LLP 882-7770 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court 'modified' its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: Sustained as to Counts 1, 2 and 3 based on Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudie Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 C.4th 503. Plaintiff has not alleged, and it does not appear, that plaintiff can allege that this defendant owed plaintiff the requisite duty or that this defendant was an employee/agent of one owing the duty and that this defendant was acting or its own financial interest. For that reason, leave to amend is not granted. The demurrer to the remaining causes of action is overruled based on Everest Investors 8 v. Whitehall Real Estate LP XI (2002) 100 CalApp,4th 1104 where "defendant" was deemed to include does absent an express limitation in the pleading. 'Answer due on 11/21/07 as to causes of action 4 thru 12.' (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike (Doe Deft From 3rd Amended Complaint) & Request For Fees & Costs
Off Calendar Nov-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP'S Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 425.16 And Request For Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $7,300.00.
APPEARANCES: Joshua Arce, Esq. 982-5100 for pltf & opposing party; Robin James, Esq. of Michelman & Robinson, LLP 882-7770 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Denied. Assuming defendant has established its initial burden of proof, plaintiff has set forth facts establishing that plaintiff has a probability of prevailing. Causes of action 4 through 12 are viable as to this defendant absent evidence to the contrary. Answer due on 11/21/07 as to causes of action 4 thru 12.
